Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment. Mildly elevated ALT may indicate hepatic involvement. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), supporting renal dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
